title: [ipv6_addr.c:99] pubkey_to_iid uses first 64 bits without MAC formatting
description:
Gap found: IID derivation output format not verified.

Why this was missed: Focus was on security of SHA-256 truncation, not format.

The actual bug: Line 99 copies first 8 bytes of SHA-256 hash to IID:
  memcpy(iid, hash, 8);

Then line 115 clears the U/L bit:
  iid[0] &= ~UL_BIT;

This produces a valid IID, but the comment about 'RFC 7343 (ORCHID) approach' (line 98) may not be accurate. RFC 7343 ORCHIDs use a specific format with hash-prefix and hash-index bits.

The actual derivation is simpler:
1. SHA-256(pubkey)
2. Take first 8 bytes
3. Clear U/L bit (locally administered)

This is valid for LICHEN but calling it 'ORCHID approach' is misleading. ORCHID has more structure.

The derivation IS safe for IID generation - just the reference is imprecise.

Priority 4 - documentation accuracy.
close_reason: Fixed: Updated comment in ipv6_addr.c to clarify that while the approach is INSPIRED by RFC 7343 ORCHID, it is NOT a compliant ORCHID (missing prefix/hash-index structure). Derivation is valid for LICHEN internal use.
